recentDecision.title: Hydra Council: ## OBJECTIVE: Review and improve SideQuest Skill System v3 Design You are reviewing a s...
recentDecision.rationale: v3 is directionally strong but still violates its own rule in many nodes: a large subset remains buff-first (most notably in Shutterbug, Diplomat, Trailrunner, Architect, Seeker, and Vanguard). Convert buff-only nodes into capability unlocks and keep XP/stat boosts as secondary riders only. Missing wow-factor candidates to add: Ghost Path Replay (Wayfinder), Quest Sonar Pulse (Wayfinder), AR Clue Overlay (Shutterbug), Time-Lapse Challenge mode (Shutterbug), Live Cipher Wheel (Codebreaker), Relay Hand-off (Trailrunner), Logic Blocks for branching conditions (Architect), Echo Hunt resurrector (Seeker), and Formation Orders (Vanguard). Balance is close at 80/168, but specialization is undercut by cheap root/branch access and uncapped synergies (especially Party Explorer scaling). Prestige quests rate 8/10 (strong), cross-tree synergies rate 6/10 (high potential but currently abusable).

latestHandoff.summary:
Hydra Council assignment for Codex 5.3.
You are the implementation specialist. You receive precise task specs with exact file paths, function signatures, and definitions of done. Execute the implementation efficiently. Do not redesign — follow the spec. Report exactly what you changed.
Objective: ## OBJECTIVE: Review and improve SideQuest Skill System v3 Design

You are reviewing a skill tree redesign for SideQuest, a location-based mobile RPG. A full v3 design doc exists at docs/design/SKILL_SYSTEM_V3_DESIGN.md. READ IT FIRST.

## CONTEXT
- Current v2: 8 trees, 64 skills, mostly passive XP percentage boosts. 13 of 16 active skills unimplemented.
- v3 Goal: Skills should UNLOCK NEW CAPABILITIES, not just stat buffs.
- Founder vision: MapleStory job advancements, Skyrim meaningful perks, unlocking cool toys and tools.

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS ALREADY MADE
- 8 trees stay (Wayfinder, Shutterbug, Codebreaker, Diplomat, Trailrunner, Architect, Seeker, Vanguard)
- Diamond layout stays (1 root, 2 branch, 4 leaf, 1 capstone = 8 per tree)
- Milestone system at 5/11/17/21 points per tree with celebrations
- Remote quest completion for Wayfinder endgame (1-3 per week)
- Quest creation features gated by skills
- Party GPS proxy from Vanguard tree
- Prestige quests that disable skill bonuses
- About 80 talent points at level 50 vs 168 total means forced specialization

## YOUR TASK
1. Read docs/design/SKILL_SYSTEM_V3_DESIGN.md thoroughly
2. Critique: Which skills genuinely unlock capabilities vs disguised stat buffs?
3. Propose improvements to any weak skills
4. Suggest 5-10 wow factor skills we might be missing
5. Evaluate balance and talent point budget
6. Flag technical feasibility concerns
7. Rate the prestige quest and cross-tree synergy systems

Return structured JSON with your analysis.
Consensus: v3 is directionally strong but still violates its own rule in many nodes: a large subset remains buff-first (most notably in Shutterbug, Diplomat, Trailrunner, Architect, Seeker, and Vanguard). Convert buff-only nodes into capability unlocks and keep XP/stat boosts as secondary riders only. Missing wow-factor candidates to add: Ghost Path Replay (Wayfinder), Quest Sonar Pulse (Wayfinder), AR Clue Overlay (Shutterbug), Time-Lapse Challenge mode (Shutterbug), Live Cipher Wheel (Codebreaker), Relay Hand-off (Trailrunner), Logic Blocks for branching conditions (Architect), Echo Hunt resurrector (Seeker), and Formation Orders (Vanguard). Balance is close at 80/168, but specialization is undercut by cheap root/branch access and uncapped synergies (especially Party Explorer scaling). Prestige quests rate 8/10 (strong), cross-tree synergies rate 6/10 (high potential but currently abusable).
Assigned tasks:
- Capability audit + weak-skill rewrite spec (DoD: Files: docs/design/SKILL_SYSTEM_V3_CAPABILITY_AUDIT.md, docs/design/SKILL_SYSTEM_V3_DESIGN.md. Steps: 1) classify all 64 skills as capability/mixed/buff-only, 2) provide replacement effect text for every buff-only skill, 3) add 5-10 wow-factor skills with target tree/tier. Done when the revised design has zero buff-only skills.) [Objectives 1-4 need a precise classification of which skills are true unlocks versus disguised numeric buffs.]
- Guardrail RPCs for prestige, remote completion, and GPS proxy (DoD: Files: supabase/migrations/201_skill_tree_v3_guardrails.sql, supabase/migrations/203_fix_get_character_active_skills_class_filter.sql. SQL signatures: calculate_quest_xp(p_character_id uuid, p_base_xp integer, p_quest_type text default null, p_distance_meters numeric default null, p_quest_radius numeric default null, p_event_id uuid default null, p_is_prestige boolean default false) returns integer; can_use_remote_completion(p_character_id uuid, p_quest_id uuid) returns jsonb; validate_party_gps_proxy(p_party_id uuid, p_leader_id uuid, p_member_id uuid, p_distance_meters numeric) returns boolean; get_character_active_skills(p_character_id uuid) returns table(...). Done when prestige fully disables bonuses and classless trees still surface active skills.) [Current mechanics can be exploited unless XP, location checks, and active-skill retrieval are patched.]
- Type surfaces for milestones, synergies, and creator limits (DoD: Files: src/types/enums.ts, src/types/index.ts. Type signatures: export type PassiveEffectType += 'remote_completion_weekly' | 'quest_creation_unlock' | 'milestone_unlock' | 'synergy_modifier'; export interface TreeMilestoneStatus { tree_id: string; points: number; unlocked_5: boolean; unlocked_11: boolean; unlocked_17: boolean; unlocked_21: boolean; }; export interface SkillSynergyStatus { synergy_key: string; is_active: boolean; trees: string[]; metadata: Record<string, unknown>; }; export interface QuestCreationLimits { max_steps: number; max_active_quests: number; feature_flags: Record<string, boolean>; }. Done when new RPC responses are fully typed without any-casts.) [The app cannot consume new RPCs safely without explicit types.]
- Hook + skills UI integration for v3 systems (DoD: Files: src/hooks/useCharacterSkills.ts, app/skills.tsx, src/components/SkillTreeView.tsx, src/components/SkillUnlockModal.tsx, src/components/ActiveSkillsPanel.tsx, src/components/SkillMilestonesPanel.tsx (new), src/components/SkillSynergyPanel.tsx (new). Function signatures: useCharacterSkills(character: Character | null): UseCharacterSkillsReturn with milestones: TreeMilestoneStatus[], synergies: SkillSynergyStatus[], creationLimits: QuestCreationLimits | null; refreshSkills(options?: { includeMeta?: boolean }): Promise<void>. Done when skills screen shows 5/11/17/21 milestones, discovered synergies, and feature gates.) [Milestones, synergies, and prestige status must be visible in the skills UX to deliver the intended fantasy.]
Open questions:
- to human: For Party Explorer, do we cap synergy as flat +50m total, or switch to +10m per Wayfinder point with a hard cap at +150m?
- to human: Should Prestige mode disable party buffs and title bonuses as well, or only skill-derived bonuses?
- to human: Should remote completion weekly charges be hard-reset each week with no rollover, and tied to location-visit proof?

The Milestone and Quest Creator Skill Gate systems are well-conceived and directly address the core design goals. However, the design violates its own primary rule: 'Explicitly banned: Skills that are ONLY '+X% XP from Y quest type''. Numerous skills across multiple trees (Shutt...

Missing wow-factor candidates to add: Ghost Path Replay (Wayfinder), Quest Sonar Pulse (Wayfinder), AR Clue Overlay (Shutterbug), Time-Lapse Challenge mode (Shutterbug), Live Cipher Wheel (Co...

This failure suggests the planning model may be overly tuned to complex, domain-specific problems (like the SideQuest V3 design) and is brittle when faced with simple, direct system commands.\",\n  \"improvements\": [\n    {\n      \"step\": 1,\n      \"description\": \"Formulate a concrete, verifiable objective for the smoke test, such as 'Verify core agent command loop and file system access.'\"\n    },\n    {\n      \"step\": 2,\n      \"description\": \"Delegate a simple, atomic task to a specific agent to execute this objective. Do not analyze other files or perform other actions.\",\n  \"revised_risks\": [\n    {\n      \"id\": \"RISK-01\",\n      \"severity\": \"CRITICAL\",\n      \"description\": \"The planning agent's failure on a simple directive suggests a lack of robustness.
